Tool_hra_Description: Calculate HRA exemption under Section 10(13A). Metro vs non-metro rules.
Tool_hra_LearnText: House Rent Allowance exemption is the least of three amounts. It applies only under the old tax regime when you live in rented housing.
Tool_hra_FormulaNote: Metro cities (Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai): 50% of basic. Other cities: 40%. Rent receipts / landlord PAN may be needed if rent exceeds ₹1 lakh/year.
Tool_hra_LabelMetro: I live in a metro city
Tool_hra_MetroHint: Metro = Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai for HRA rules.
Tool_hra_ExampleStep_5: Exemption = min of three = ₹2,40,000; taxable HRA = ₹0.
Tool_hra_Step_1: Note actual HRA from Form 16 / payslip.
Tool_hra_Step_2: Compute rent paid minus 10% of basic.
Tool_hra_Step_4: Exemption is the least of the three; rest is taxable.
Tool_hra_Info_Purpose: Find how much of your HRA is tax-exempt under Section 10(13A).
Tool_hra_Info_WhyKnow: Many salaried employees overpay tax by not claiming the correct HRA exemption — or claim too much and face notices.
Tool_hra_Info_RealLife: Old-regime tax planning, rent vs buy decisions, salary restructuring.
Tool_hra_Info_Goal_3: Know taxable HRA left after exemption
Tool_hra_Info_Alt_Salary_When: When you need full CTC to in-hand.
Tool_hra_Info_Alt_Salary_Cons: Uses simplified HRA inside tax estimate.
Tool_hra_Info_Alt_80gg_When: When you pay rent but do not receive HRA.
Tool_hra_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows which of the three limits is binding — so you know what to change (rent, basic, or HRA).
Tool_hra_Info_Motto: Claim the exemption the law actually allows.
Tool_gst_Description: Add or remove GST at 5%, 12%, 18%, or 28%. CGST/SGST/IGST split.
Tool_gst_LearnText: GST can be added on top of a base price (exclusive) or extracted from a tax-inclusive price. Intra-state supplies split GST into CGST + SGST; inter-state uses IGST.
Tool_gst_FormulaNote: Common slabs: 0%, 5%, 12%, 18%, 28%. Always confirm the HSN/SAC rate for your product or service.
Tool_gst_SupplyHint: Same state = CGST+SGST. Different state = IGST.
Tool_gst_ExampleStep_5: If the price was inclusive ₹11,800, base = 11,800 / 1.18 = ₹10,000.
Tool_gst_Step_3: Split into CGST+SGST or show IGST.
Tool_gst_Step_4: Use taxable value on the tax invoice.
Tool_gst_Info_Purpose: Quickly add or remove GST and see the CGST/SGST/IGST split.
Tool_gst_Info_WhyKnow: Wrong GST on invoices causes customer disputes and compliance issues. A clear split helps bookkeeping.
Tool_gst_Info_RealLife: Quoting customers, checking bills, freelancers raising invoices.
Tool_gst_Info_Goal_1: Add GST to a base price
Tool_gst_Info_Goal_2: Extract GST from an inclusive price
Tool_gst_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra handles both add and remove modes with an explicit CGST/SGST/IGST split.
Tool_gst_Info_Motto: Price it right — tax inclusive or exclusive.
Tool_salary_Description: CTC to in-hand breakdown. HRA, PF, gratuity, and new vs old regime tax.
Tool_salary_LearnText: CTC is not take-home. Employer PF and gratuity sit inside CTC; employee PF, professional tax, and income tax come out of gross to leave in-hand salary.
Tool_salary_FormulaNote: Educational estimate. PF wage ceiling ₹15,000 applies when “PF on actual” is off. Professional tax uses a simplified Maharashtra-style slab. Tax uses FinYatra’s FY 2025-26 engine.
Tool_salary_RentHint: Used for old-regime HRA exemption inside the tax estimate.
Tool_salary_LabelMetro: Metro city (for HRA in old regime)
Tool_salary_ExampleStep_1: CTC ₹12,00,000 with ~40% basic and 50% HRA of basic.
Tool_salary_ExampleStep_2: Employer PF and gratuity are carved out of CTC.
Tool_salary_ExampleStep_3: Employee PF (12%) and professional tax reduce monthly pay.
Tool_salary_ExampleStep_4: Income tax is estimated under the selected regime.
Tool_salary_ExampleStep_5: In-hand = gross − employee PF − PT − tax.
Tool_salary_Step_1: Split CTC into basic, HRA, special allowance, employer costs.
Tool_salary_Step_2: Compute employee PF and professional tax.
Tool_salary_Step_3: Estimate annual income tax for the chosen regime.
Tool_salary_Step_4: Subtract deductions from gross for monthly in-hand.
Tool_salary_Info_Purpose: Convert annual CTC into an estimated monthly take-home salary.
Tool_salary_Info_WhyKnow: Offer letters quote CTC. Your lifestyle runs on in-hand. Knowing the gap avoids overspending after a job switch.
Tool_salary_Info_RealLife: Comparing job offers, negotiating basic %, choosing tax regime.
Tool_salary_Info_Goal_2: See PF, PT, and tax drag
Tool_salary_Info_Goal_3: Compare old vs new regime take-home
Tool_salary_Info_Alt_Tax_When: For a detailed old vs new regime comparison.
Tool_salary_Info_Alt_Tax_Cons: Does not model monthly payslip components.
Tool_salary_Info_Alt_Hra_Cons: Does not show full CTC breakup.
Tool_salary_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows CTC components and monthly deductions side by side — not just a single take-home number.
Tool_salary_Info_Motto: Know what actually hits your bank account.
